title: FlatList Props and Performance
description: Customise your list with headers, footers, separators, and optimise rendering.

slides.title: Enhancing Your List ✨
slides.content:
# Useful FlatList Props

* `ListHeaderComponent` – a component rendered at the top of the list.
* `ListFooterComponent` – at the bottom.
* `ItemSeparatorComponent` – rendered between items.
* `horizontal` – make the list scroll horizontally.
* `numColumns` – display items in a grid (multiple columns).

All these props accept either a React component or a function returning an element.

slides.title: Header, Footer, and Separators
slides.content: A FlatList with a header, footer, and separators:
slides.codeSnippet:
<FlatList
  data={DATA}
  keyExtractor={(item) => item.id}
  renderItem={({ item }) => <Text>{item.title}</Text>}
  ListHeaderComponent={<Text style={{ fontSize: 24 }}>My List</Text>}
  ListFooterComponent={<Text>End of list</Text>}
  ItemSeparatorComponent={() => <View style={{ height: 1, backgroundColor: '#ccc' }} />}
/>

slides.title: Grid Layout 🧩

quizData.question: Which prop would you use to render something between every item in a FlatList?
quizData.explanation: `ItemSeparatorComponent` renders a component between each item.

slides.title: Performance Optimisation 🚀
slides.content:
## Making FlatList Even Faster

For very long lists, you can provide hints to improve performance:

* `initialNumToRender` – how many items to render initially (default 10). Increase if you want to see more items immediately.
* `maxToRenderPerBatch` – how many items to render per batch when scrolling (default 10).
* `windowSize` – number of screen heights worth of items to render (default 21). Higher values keep more off‑screen items mounted, which can help with smooth scrolling but uses more memory.
* `removeClippedSubviews` – set to `true` to detach off‑screen views (may improve memory, but can cause issues with absolute positioning).
* `getItemLayout` – if you know the size of each item, provide it to skip measuring.

slides.title: Using getItemLayout
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: If your list items have dynamic heights, consider using `onLayout` to measure them and store the layout info. But for most cases, the default settings are sufficient.
